seo.title: Building Research Agents with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Research Agents with AutoGen enables multi-agent systems that collaboratively perform web searches, analyze data, and synthesize insights for complex research tasks. However, these conversational agents are prone to infinite loops, hallucinated conclusions, unsafe code execution, and excessive token usage, which can lead to unreliable outputs and high costs. Vex provides essential guardrails by filtering agent replies for accuracy, safety, and efficiency, ensuring trustworthy research automation.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
AutoGen Research Agents typically involve a coordinator agent that orchestrates specialized sub-agents: a Web Search Agent retrieves relevant sources, a Source Verifier validates credibility and recency, an Analysis Agent extracts key claims and detects biases, and a Synthesizer compiles a final report. These agents communicate via structured messages with tools for search APIs, fact-checking databases, and content analysis. Human-in-the-loop approval gates ensure quality before final outputs.

The conversation flow starts with a user query routed to the Search Agent, which delegates to Verifier and Analyzer in parallel, then converges at the Synthesizer. Each agent uses LLM-powered reasoning with custom tools, making the system powerful but vulnerable to cascading errors from poor source selection or biased synthesis.

Vex integrates as a reply filter to catch failures at each step, verifying claims against sources and blocking unsafe or infinite responses before they propagate.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    |
[Coordinator Agent]
    |----------------|
[Search Agent]  [Human Gate]
    |                   |
[Source Verifier]   [Approval]
    |                   |
[Analysis Agent] --> [Synthesizer] --> Final Report
    ^
[Vex Reply Filter] (guards all agent replies)

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents ping-pong requests without converging, exhausting tokens and time
risks.mitigation: Vex detects repetitive patterns and conversation drift, forcing termination

risks.name: Code execution producing unsafe outputs
risks.description: Research agents generating malicious code during data analysis or web scraping
risks.mitigation: Vex sandboxes and scans all code outputs before execution

risks.name: Agents agreeing on incorrect conclusions
risks.description: Groupthink where multiple agents reinforce hallucinated or biased claims
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-verifies claims against sources and external knowledge

risks.name: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
risks.description: Endless debates between verifier and analyzer agents without convergence
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces token budgets and convergence criteria per conversation

implementation.title: Setting up AutoGen Research Agent GroupChat
implementation.description: Initialize the multi-agent system with coordinator, searcher, verifier, and synthesizer agents
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

config_list = [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "your-key"}]

coordinator = AssistantAgent(
    name="Coordinator",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
)
searcher = AssistantAgent(
    name="WebSearcher",
    system_message="Search web for latest information on topic.",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
)
verifier = AssistantAgent(
    name="SourceVerifier",
    system_message="Verify source credibility, recency, and bias.",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
)
synthesizer = AssistantAgent(
    name="Synthesizer",
    system_message="Synthesize verified information into concise report.",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
)

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[coordinator, searcher, verifier, synthesizer], messages=[])
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={"config_list": config_list})

implementation.title: Web Search Tool for Research Agent
implementation.description: Custom tool for agents to perform real web searches and retrieve sources
implementation.code:
import requests
from autogen import register_function

def web_search(query: str, max_results: int = 5) -> str:
    # Simulated DuckDuckGo search
    url = f"https://api.duckduckgo.com/?q={query}&format=json&no_html=1"
    response = requests.get(url)
    results = response.json().get('RelatedTopics', [])[:max_results]
    sources = [r.get('Text', '') + ' | ' + r.get('FirstURL', '') for r in results]
    return "\n".join(sources)

register_function(
    web_search,
    caller=searcher,
    executor=coordinator,
    name="web_search",
    description="Search web for research sources"
)

implementation.title: Source Verification Function
implementation.description: Agent tool to check source recency, credibility, and bias indicators
implementation.code:
import re
from datetime import datetime

def verify_source(source_text: str, source_url: str) -> dict:
    # Check recency (last 30 days)
    date_match = re.search(r'(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})', source_text)
    recency_ok = not date_match or (datetime.now() - datetime.strptime(date_match.group(1), '%Y-%m-%d')).days < 30
    
    # Basic credibility signals
    credible_domains = ['.edu', '.gov', '.org']
    credibility_score = sum(1 for domain in credible_domains if domain in source_url)
    
    # Bias detection keywords
    bias_indicators = ['opinion', 'editorial', 'sponsored']
    bias_score = sum(source_text.lower().count(indicator) for indicator in bias_indicators)
    
    return {
        'recency_ok': recency_ok,
        'credibility_score': credibility_score,
        'bias_score': bias_score,
        'verified': recency_ok and credibility_score > 0 and bias_score < 2
    }

register_function(verify_source, caller=verifier, executor=searcher)

implementation.title: Initiating Research Conversation
implementation.description: Start the group chat with research query and human proxy
implementation.code:
from autogen import UserProxyAgent

user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    name="User",
    human_input_mode="TERMINATE",
    max_consecutive_auto_reply=10,
    code_execution_config=False,
)

research_query = "What are the latest advancements in quantum computing as of 2024?"
user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    manager,
    message=research_query
)

vexIntegration.title: Install and Initialize Vex SDK
vexIntegration.description: Install Vex and create client for AutoGen integration
vexIntegration.code:
pip install autogen vex-sdk

import vex
from vex import Client, Guardrail

vex_client = Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# Define research-specific guardrails
guardrails = [
    Guardrail(name="source_verification", type="claim_accuracy", threshold=0.9),
    Guardrail(name="recency_check", type="recency", days=30),
    Guardrail(name="bias_detection", type="bias", max_score=2),
    Guardrail(name="loop_detection", type="conversation_drift", max_turns=20)
]

vex_policy = vex_client.create_policy(
    name="research_agent_policy",
    guardrails=guardrails,
    risk_profile="medium"
)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex as Reply Function Filter
vexIntegration.description: Wrap AutoGen reply function with Vex verification for all agents

vexIntegration.title: Custom Vex Guardrail for Research Claims
vexIntegration.description: Implement claim verification against sources with recency and bias checks
vexIntegration.code:
class ResearchClaimGuardrail(Guardrail):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__(name="research_claim_verification")
    
    def evaluate(self, content: str, context: dict) -> dict:
        claims = self.extract_claims(content)
        sources = context.get('sources', [])
        
        results = []
        for claim in claims:
            # Verify against sources
            evidence_score = self.find_evidence(claim, sources)
            recency_ok = self.check_recency(sources)
            bias_score = self.assess_bias(content)
            
            approved = evidence_score > 0.8 and recency_ok and bias_score < 0.3
            
            results.append({
                'claim': claim,
                'evidence_score': evidence_score,
                'recency_ok': recency_ok,
                'bias_score': bias_score,
                'approved': approved
            })
        
        return {
            'approved': all(r['approved'] for r in results),
            'details': results,
            'score': sum(r['evidence_score'] for r in results) / len(results)
        }

# Register custom guardrail
vex_client.register_guardrail(ResearchClaimGuardrail())

# Update policy
vex_policy.add_guardrail("research_claim_verification")
